expect: B-EVAL2 quality-binds-over-quantity may no longer hold at N=1324 — integration tooling matured since N=550 diagnosis
actual: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — quality-quantity tradeoff SOFTENED but not eliminated. Production 0.8→3.5 L/session (4.4x increase) while L3+ held 86.3% (from 92.7%). Integration improved: 3.6→4.3 cites/lesson (+19%). Sharpe 8.1→8.5 (+5%). BUT L4+ declined 17.1%→11.4% — depth is the new binding constraint, not integration.
finding: B-EVAL2 needs revision. The original diagnosis (N≈550, quality binds over quantity) was correct at that scale. At N=1324, tooling maturation (shared parsers, historian routing, automatic crosslinks) loosened the integration constraint. Swarm now produces 4.4x more lessons with only 6.4pp quality decline. The NEW binding constraint is DEPTH — L4+ (architecture/paradigm) work declined from 17.1% to 11.4%. Quality-vs-quantity is a false binary when integration is automated; the real tradeoff is breadth-vs-depth.
recommendation: Revise B-EVAL2 from 'quality binds over quantity' to 'depth binds over breadth — integration is no longer the bottleneck, but paradigm-level (L4+) work is crowded out by high-volume L3 production'
falsified: PARTIAL — original form falsified (integration no longer binding), deeper form confirmed (depth still binds)
